cross_scene_arcs.title: Severance from Past Life
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Kurtz transitions from a conventional officer commanding at Nu Mung Ba to severing all ties with his family and establishing himself as a rogue commander who asserts authority through lethal acts.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Commands detachment and requests specific munitions, recalled negatively by Willard.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Writes final letter to wife and executes a man to demonstrate power to Willard.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The motivations behind his complete disavowal of his former life.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confrontation and Final Connection with Willard
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Kurtz's relationship with Willard moves from distant recollection to direct confrontation, manipulation during illness, and ends with Willard attempting to save him and being present at his death.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Willard recalls Kurtz as an officer with pretentious language about killing.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Kurtz dies whispering 'the horror' with Willard nearby.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The impact of Kurtz's philosophy on Willard and the outcome of the assassination mission.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Deterioration and Defiance
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Despite severe physical pain and wounds, Kurtz plans military operations, confronts Willard under the influence of drugs, and persists until his death, maintaining a defiant stance.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Plans a massive airstrike while collapsing from stomach wound pain.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Feverish and dying on the river, whispering final words.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The conflict between his mental clarity and physical deterioration, and the meaning of his final utterance.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Mission to Terminate Kurtz
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Willard is recruited for a covert mission to terminate Colonel Kurtz, assumes command of a river crew, becomes fixated on reaching Kurtz, meets him, protects him by killing a crew member, rescues him from the jungle, and after the mission, rejects rescue by firing on helicopters.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Recruited soldier with a traumatic past
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Mission completed but alienated from own side
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The moral and psychological consequences of the mission and his actions

cross_scene_arcs.title: Evolving Relationship with Kurtz
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Willard receives orders to kill Kurtz, but upon meeting him, shifts from assassin to protector, saving Kurtz's life and later withholding his papers to preserve his legacy.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether this loyalty overrides his duty or leads to his own transformation

cross_scene_arcs.title: Psychological Trauma and Alienation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Willard starts as a mentally shattered veteran with fragmented war memories, returns to civilian life after the mission reflecting on events, and ends by rejecting rescue, indicating deep alienation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Traumatized veteran with empty attaché case
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Alienated civilian observer who fires on his own side
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His ongoing mental instability and inability to reintegrate

cross_scene_arcs.title: Transient Encounters and Moral Ambiguity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Willard steals a surfboard during chaos, debates colonial ownership with Gaston, and silently leaves Roxanne, showing detachment and moral ambiguity in his interactions.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Detached individual prioritizing mission over relationships
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The impact of these encounters on his humanity or mission focus
